entry.title: Synchronization in Action – Executing the Integration Between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio
entry.content:
In the previous blog (Initial Setup – Preparing for SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio Integration), we explained the steps to start the provisioning process for a SAP Signavio connectivity Add-on. In this blog, we’ll cover how to synchronize SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM.

  1. The first step is to open the process you want to synchronize in the Process Collaboration Hub (This is crucial because if you don't do this, the synchronization option won't appear).
  2. To perform the synchronization, we need to open the process in 'Preview' mode. To enter 'Preview' mode, click on your user icon and select the 'View' option, then choose 'Preview'. This will change the screen (you’ll see the preview white/blue line ).
  3. Now, in the options menu (the three dots in the toolbar), you need to click in the option-->'Sync with SAP Cloud ALM'.
  4. A new screen will open that you need to confirm. This screen explains the revision schema and some restrictions.
  5. After the sync is complete, a confirmation button will appear to confirm the success of the activity. You can also click on the 'Open in SAP Cloud ALM' option to jump directly to the corresponding process.
  6. Now you can see the process in SAP Cloud ALM in 'Draft' status. Notice that the process has a lock icon, meaning that it needs to be maintained in the Signavio tool. If you are an administrator in SAP Cloud ALM, you will be able to unlock the process to modify it in CALM.

To recap, this synchronization process between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM is just one small part of the complete lifecycle that needs to be defined.

Other points to consider:

  • Defining a proper lifecycle for process maintenance is crucial, understanding in which tool and who is responsible for the activities is a key definition.
  • Defining the correct integration with LeanIX to achieve greater synergy and reduce duplicated efforts.
  • The integration between SAP Cloud ALM and EnableNow is another key element to ensure a clear path for system transformation

entry.title: No Successor App available for Manage Catalog Item Recommendations (F3293)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

Good day!

Raising this request for assistance to look/find a possible successor or related app to Manage Catalog Item Recommendations that can still do the same functionality.

We appreciate any help/assistance in any way.

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No Successor App available for Manage Catalog Item Re...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

This kind of query should be raised via the SAP Communities. 

In relation to the App, when I check in the Fiori Apps Library: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3149')/S29OP
There is no mention of the App being depreciated, it is still available and valid. 

If you have further queries on an app's lifespan, functionalities or configuration please always check in the Fiori Apps Library. 

I hope this helps, 
Kind Regards, 
Lisa 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Access for users to update allocation rules in SAC
entry.content:
Hi @Vivek27 

regarding your first question

  1. What access will be needed for users to update allocation rules? Is there any other way without giving developer role?

you need planning professional role. Since Allocation steps migrated to data actions a planning standard user cannot maintain the allocation steps. It is a bit contradictive because in the legacy allocation designer a planning standard user was able to maintain those steps.

br,

thomas

entry.title: Employee is not receiving notifications from MLL, Her supervisor is receiving them overdue.
entry.content:
One employee is not receiving her MLL course overdue notifications. It appears there is an issue with her MLL profile. While other employees are receiving their notifications, she is not receiving hers. However, she does receive notifications for her direct reports. Brittney has confirmed that she used to receive these notifications before, but currently her manager is receiving her overdue email notifications.

I have checked her Outlook settings to ensure there are no rules or logic affecting this, and we have successfully sent notifications to her manually.

Could you please assist in identifying any profile changes that might prevent an employee from receiving their own notifications?
Employee ID: Brittney Blizard ‎(U773214)‎

entry.title: Re: Rental Process in SAP Field Logistics
entry.content: Hi @Aleem : You can initiate the returns of rental items using "Initiate returns by Products" App. It should create a STO/OBD to move the goods back to onshore supply base.

entry.title: How to Analyze "CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED" Issue in CPI
entry.content:
Overview:
Based on our previous support for our valued customers and partners in developing integration adapters for cloud platform integration, we’ve identified that some of our partners/customers are encountering issues while qualifying their adapters. This blog will guide you on how to resolve the "CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED" issue in integration flows.

 

How to Analyze "CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED" Issue:
This issue typically arises when a class is not properly loaded. Class loading can fail due to "Dependency issues" or "An error in the adapter code, which throws an exception during the object creation at the class level".

Step 1: Identify Which Adapter Variant is Affected
First, we need to determine whether the issue lies with the sender adapter, receiver adapter, or both. We can isolate this by creating and deploying basic integration flows with the following configurations:

Sender Adapter: Create and deploy a basic integration flow with only the sender adapter.
Receiver Adapter: Create and deploy a basic integration flow with only the receiver adapter.
Both Adapters: Create and deploy a basic integration flow with both sender and receiver adapters.

Findings from Step 1:
Based on the results, we can narrow down the issue to the specific code in our adapter:
If the issue is on the sender side: Further investigate the consumer component of the integration adapter.
If the issue is on the receiver side: Further investigate the producer component of the integration adapter.
If the issue is on both sides: The issue is likely within the endpoint class.


Step 2: Add Additional Logs to Adapter Code
To understand the root cause, we need to add more logs to the adapter code.

For sender adapter issues:
Move all consumer class-level object initializations to the constructor.
Add logs after every initialization to trace the execution flow.

For receiver adapter issues:
Move all producer class-level object initializations to the constructor.
Add logs after every initialization to trace the execution flow.

For issues in both variants:
Move all endpoint class-level object initializations to the constructor.
Add logs after every initialization to trace the execution flow.
By adding these logs, you can pinpoint where the failure is occurring and resolve the issue effectively.

 

Thanks

entry.title: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content:
I have a customer who is currently using basic authentication (ID and passwords). It seems like we have 2 articles regarding basic authentication’s depreciation timeline.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/55e0c3af883748749c308f3b719a4c01.html

And

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/1083c36fc3c34515a37266ce52811f7e.html

 

Which is the correct Emd of maintenance date? June or November?

If the customer experience some difficulties regarding their login after June, will SAP product team take care?

entry.title: JSON Message to change field value to null via Groovy Script
entry.content:
Hello, I'm still new to groovy scripting and I would like to achieve the following output.

Example

Input:

 

{
	"Book": {
		"Title": {
			"@nil": "true"
		},
		"Author": {
			"@nil": "true"
		},
		"year": "1960",
		"isbn": "9780061120084"
	}
}

 

Output:

 

	{
		"Book": {
			"Title":null,
			"Author": null,
			"year": "1960",
			"isbn": "9780061120084"
		}
	}

 

I need to change all the fields that has value = {"@nil": "true"} to null. How can I achieve this via groovy script? would really appreciate your help on this. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Problems while upload many items from offline store
entry.content: I am using MDK Version 24.7

entry.title: Re: No batch input data for screen SAPLSPO1 0500
entry.content:
(04.02.2025):--

I did a solution for my company. The same problem occurred in transaction CS20. For example, there was a function: call function 'CS_BI_BOM_CHANGE_BATCH_INPUT' --tcode_mode's value  was 'N', then I just changed 'E'. It worked for us.

Maya

entry.title: Introducing New Safety Data Sheet Features
entry.content:
Here are the highlights for the newest features for Safety Data Sheet Management:

  • SDS Generation extended to include sections 9, 11 and 12
  • New Business Event between Partners and SAP BTP: Request Data Calculation
  • New API’s to move data from legacy systems into SAP S/4HANA for product compliance
    • GHS Classification
    • Handling and Storage
    • Safety Related Properties
  • Versioning of GHS assessments for substances
  • Compliance Requirement topic to group requirements together when based on the same compliance pattern
  • Waste Code compliance requirements versions now have aspect definitions available
  • New Aerosol Labeling app

 

In this post we will go through the system and see how to maintain and review basic data for our example industrial cleaner.

Industrial Cleaner Example

Here is the Work View, where we have an overview of the processing status of the different data under each section.

 

From the Work View we can navigate to the different sections for specific information including History and Admin Data.

 

We can also check our information at the substance level via the analytical composition.

 

The new Business Event on requesting data calculations enables partners to consume business events running data calculations with public API’s to read and write the dependent datasets.

Along with the new Compliance Requirement for the EU Waste Code, you can see here the new user interface for setting the detailed information.

For Aerosol Labeling as new app variant, under the Manage Regulatory Data app, has been developed to manage data for unpackaged products and raw materials. This accompanies a new compliance requirement for Aerosol Labeling based on the Regulatory Data Assessment compliance pattern. For more information check this out.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Allocation Step - Planning Standard User Maintenance
entry.content:
I noticed a conflict about allocation maintenance in SAP Analytics Cloud. In the user set-up

SAC allows to non-Planning Professional users to create or maintain allocation step.

 

But in the Data Action, SAC allows only Planning Professional users to maintain/Save it.

So, how can a Planning Standard user can maintain an Allocation step if the Data Action can’t be saved from a Planning Standard user?

entry.content:

Need to add zfields in the header portion as per business requirement. I want to make a copy of "BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE" to ''ZBAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE''. In the header portion 3 custom fields need to add.

Function group MB_BUS2017 copy as ZMB_BUS2017.For Function module am I give Z against all old function module. Please guided me step by step guideline to complete the task or any link.

Customs fields data kept in Ztable. Using BAPI ZBAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE user complete GRN from Apps.

Am I copy standard BAPI and create a ZBAPI or using standard BAPI added custom fields to BAPI2017_GM_HEAD_01 (which is added in header portion in mIGO).

Need guideline to complete the job.

Custom fields in MIGO:

Custom Tables where data saved:

Thanks in advance.

Regards-
Rajib

entry.title: Re: Mandatory Storage Bin Type for Bulk Storage: Reasons...
entry.content:
Hello @Mounirho 

Storage bin types together with handling unit types define bulk structures - SPRO SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Goods Receipt Process -> Strategies -> Putaway Rules -> Storage Behavior: Bulk Storage -> Define Bulk Structures. A bulk structure defines how many HUs fit into a bin. You can't define that without a bin type. Therefore bulk storage type without storage bin types doesn't make sense.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩

Click in the person icon, not in your initial and go to my content:

Javier

entry.title: Re: what is service request for system refresh on sap portal
entry.content: Use ECS - Homogeneous System Copy template , and Choose 'System Refresh' or 'System Copy' scenario

entry.title: Re: Can we customize the process template of workflow in...
entry.content:
hi Rahul,

You can look at the blog post MDG Cloud Ready Mode: Custom Workflow template in ... - SAP Community , this is how I had achieved custom workflow in SBPA and integrated with cloud ready mode.

This is a very basic example; however, it differs from the standard help document which is prescribed.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: Subject Name Identifiier in IAS
entry.content:
Hi tskwin  

I recommend to check the config for 'Identity Federation' in the trust config from IAS to Entra.
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/integration-configure-identity-federation 
If Identity Federation is switched off, then IAS will simply forward the assertion provided by the corp. IdP to the application.
If however Identity Federation is on and IAS user store to be used, then IAS will perform a user lookup based on the subject name identifier sent by Entra and 'compile' a new assertion based on the application config in IAS.
Marko

entry.title: Re: Suppress Custom uibb in sap mdg Customer

entry.title: Re: Update and Create Events in Table Maintenance Generator
entry.content:
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'AENAM' OF STRUCTURE <vim_total_struc> TO <fs_field>.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
      <fs_field> = sy-uname.
    ENDIF.

Hari

entry.title: Webi report is failing while refreshing with no memory error but works without issue when refreshing
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am facing strange issue that when i schedule one SAP BusinessObjects - Web Intelligence (WebI) report it runs for around 1 hour and then fails with memory error in backend BW (SAP Business Warehouse) system. while checking in BW i can see the memory dump

Now surprising part is if run the same report manually in web intelligence with similar parameter it runs without any issue and generates the output in about 10-11 minutes. 

Here i fail to understand why it is behavious in this way.

Anyone have faced such issue and know the possible root cause?

Additional Information:

SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 4 Patch 6

Version : 14.3.4.5014.6

Scheduling output is CSV Archive

entry.title: Sales KIT
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement to create a sales order with sales kits. In this scenario, my client wants to sell product A. However, product A needs to be sold together with 3 product components. This case can be defined like that:

Example:

Product A 

@         Product A.1

         Product A.2

         Product A.3

Initially, i thought about creating sales bom. However, my client can have more than 100 different combinaisons. For this reason, we are not going with the sales bom.

Could you please propose me other solutions which i can explore? Is it possible to use delivery group in this case? If yes, how can i configure the item category by including the parameter for the delivery group?

Thanks

 @Lakshmipathi 

@jignesh_mehta3

entry.title: Re: Defects Creation and Problem Solving by using 8-D Methodology in RISE with SAP Public Cloud 2302
entry.content:
Hi  Joe,

Nice Blog & its very useful to every one.

Thanks,

Hari Haran M.

entry.title: Disable user in ABAP after deletion in EntraID
entry.content:
For audit reasons, you do not want to delete users in the ABAP system when a person leaves the organization. So it is desirable to lock that useraccount instead of a deletion. 

I am reading the users from EntraID to IAS and push them to S/4. If a user is now deleted in EntraID, that user gets deleted in S/4. I could set the parameter to not delete the user in ABAP, but than I will still be active, which is an issue with the license count in S/4

 

So, is there a way to lock the user in S/4 when it gets deleted from EntraID/IAS?

 

thanks

Sander

entry.title: End routine logic not working with multiple record load via dtp.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We've written a end routine logic for calculating sales value %, sales quantity % & Average price. But if we execute the dtp with a single record this will give correct result. but for more records scenario this calculation not working.


For calculating sales value %, we need to take the sum of division wise net value sum based on bill num.
Also we need to take the sum of material group wise net value sum based on bill num.
Sales value % formula = ( material group wise net value sum / division wise net value sum ) * 100
above logic is used for sales quantity % also.
For Average price = sum of net price / count based on division

DATA : LV_TOTALVALUE_MG TYPE P DECIMALS 2,
       LV_TOTALQTY_MG TYPE I,
       LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV TYPE P DECIMALS 2,
       LV_TOTALQTY_DIV TYPE I,
       LV_AVSELLPRICE_MG TYPE P DECIMALS 2,
       LV_AVSELLPRICE_DIV TYPE P DECIMALS 2,
       LV_CNT TYPE I.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ZSOTABLE,
BILL_NUM TYPE /BI0/OIBILL_NUM,
BILL_ITEM TYPE /BI0/OIBILL_ITEM,
ZZREGION TYPE /BI0/OIREGION,
ZZCOUNTRY TYPE /BI0/OICOUNTRY,
END OF ZSOTABLE.
TYPES: BEGIN OF ZSOTABLE1,
/BIC/ZZBILLNUM TYPE /BIC/OIZZBILLNUM,
* /BIC/ZZBILITM TYPE /BI0/OIBILL_ITEM,
PRICE_DATE TYPE /BI0/OIPRICE_DATE,
* NET_PRICE TYPE /BI0/OINET_PRICE,
END OF ZSOTABLE1.
TYPES: BEGIN OF ZSOTABLE2,
/BIC/ZZSTATE TYPE /BIC/OIZZSTATE,
ZZCOUNTRY TYPE /BIC/OIZZCOUNTRY,
ZZSTATCOD TYPE /BIC/OIZZSTATCOD,
/BIC/ZZREGION TYPE /BIC/OIZZREGION,

END OF ZSOTABLE2.
DATA : ZSORDERS TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZSOTABLE,
WA_ZSORDERS TYPE ZSOTABLE. " internal table for /BIC/AZ_D_BITM00

DATA : ZSORDERS1 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZSOTABLE1,
WA_ZSORDERS1 TYPE ZSOTABLE1. "internal table for /BIC/AZSD_O1000


DATA : ZSORDERS2 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZSOTABLE2,
WA_ZSORDERS2 TYPE ZSOTABLE2.
SELECT DOC_NUMBER
DOC_ITEM
REGION
COUNTRY
FROM /BIC/AZ_D_BITM00 INTO TABLE ZSORDERS FOR ALL
"From Billing Item (Granual Level) Lookup DSO
ENTRIES IN RESULT_PACKAGE WHERE
DOC_NUMBER = RESULT_PACKAGE-BILL_NUM AND
DOC_ITEM = RESULT_PACKAGE-BILL_ITEM.
SELECT /BIC/ZZBILLNUM
* /BIC/ZZBILITM
PRICE_DATE
* NET_PRICE
FROM /BIC/AZSD_O1000 INTO TABLE ZSORDERS1 FOR ALL
" From Loss of Sales Lookup DSO
ENTRIES IN RESULT_PACKAGE WHERE
/BIC/ZZBILLNUM = RESULT_PACKAGE-BILL_NUM.
SELECT /BIC/ZZSTATE
/BIC/ZZCOUNTRY
/BIC/ZZSTATCOD
/BIC/ZZREGION
FROM /BIC/AZSD_O1300 INTO TABLE ZSORDERS2.
SORT RESULT_PACKAGE BY BILL_NUM BILL_ITEM DIVISION MATL_GROUP.
DELETE RESULT_PACKAGE WHERE INV_QTY is INITIAL.
SORT ZSORDERS BY BILL_NUM BILL_ITEM ASCENDING.

SORT ZSORDERS1 BY /BIC/ZZBILLNUM ASCENDING.

SORT ZSORDERS2 BY ZZSTATCOD ASCENDING.
LOOP AT RESULT_PACKAGE INTO WA_PACKAGE.
READ TABLE ZSORDERS1 INTO WA_ZSORDERS1 WITH KEY
/BIC/ZZBILLNUM = WA_PACKAGE-BILL_NUM.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
ENDIF.

MODIFY RESULT_PACKAGE from WA_PACKAGE.

CLEAR WA_PACKAGE.

ENDLOOP.
CLEAR IT_RESULT_PACKAGE[].
IT_RESULT_PACKAGE[] = RESULT_PACKAGE[].

""Repeat the SORT
SORT RESULT_PACKAGE BY BILL_NUM BILL_ITEM DIVISION MATL_GROUP.
SORT IT_RESULT_PACKAGE BY BILL_NUM BILL_ITEM DIVISION MATL_GROUP.
CLEAR WA_PACKAGE.
LOOP AT RESULT_PACKAGE INTO WA_PACKAGE.
READ TABLE ZSORDERS INTO WA_ZSORDERS WITH KEY
BILL_NUM = WA_PACKAGE-BILL_NUM
BILL_ITEM = WA_PACKAGE-BILL_ITEM.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZZSTATCOD =
WA_ZSORDERS-ZZREGION.
WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZZCOUNTRY =
WA_ZSORDERS-ZZCOUNTRY.

ENDIF.

READ TABLE ZSORDERS2 INTO WA_ZSORDERS2 WITH KEY
ZZSTATCOD = WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZZSTATCOD
ZZCOUNTRY = WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZZCOUNTRY.
IF SY-SUBRC = 0.
ENDIF.
LOOP AT IT_RESULT_PACKAGE INTO WA_RESULT.
IF WA_RESULT-DIVISION = WA_PACKAGE-DIVISION AND
WA_RESULT-MATL_GROUP = WA_PACKAGE-MATL_GROUP.

""For Material Grp wise Total
LV_TOTALQTY_MG = LV_TOTALQTY_MG + WA_RESULT-INV_QTY.
LV_TOTALVALUE_MG = LV_TOTALVALUE_MG + WA_RESULT-NET_VALUE.

""For Division wise Total
LV_TOTALQTY_DIV = LV_TOTALQTY_DIV + WA_RESULT-INV_QTY.
LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV = LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV + WA_RESULT-NET_VALUE.

LV_CNT = LV_CNT + 1.
LV_AVSELLPRICE_MG = LV_AVSELLPRICE_MG + WA_RESULT-NET_PRICE.

ELSE.

IF WA_RESULT-DIVISION = WA_PACKAGE-DIVISION.

LV_TOTALQTY_DIV = LV_TOTALQTY_DIV + WA_RESULT-INV_QTY.
LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV = LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV + WA_RESULT-NET_VALUE.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

CLEAR : WA_RESULT.

ENDLOOP.
IF LV_TOTALQTY_DIV NE 0.
WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZSALESQTY = ( LV_TOTALQTY_MG / LV_TOTALQTY_DIV ) * 100.

ENDIF.
IF LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV NE 0.
WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZSALESVAL = ( LV_TOTALVALUE_MG / LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV ) *
100.

ENDIF.
IF LV_CNT NE 0.
WA_PACKAGE-/BIC/ZAVGPRICE = ( LV_AVSELLPRICE_MG / LV_CNT ).

ENDIF.

MODIFY RESULT_PACKAGE FROM WA_PACKAGE.

CLEAR: LV_TOTALQTY_MG,
LV_TOTALQTY_DIV,
LV_TOTALVALUE_MG,
LV_TOTALVALUE_DIV,
LV_AVSELLPRICE_MG,
LV_CNT,
WA_PACKAGE.

ENDLOOP.

DELETE RESULT_PACKAGE WHERE BILL_NUM IS INITIAL.

entry.title: Re: Standard BAPI BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE copy
entry.content:
Why didn't you use the EXTENSIONIN parameter of the BAPI and process this data with an implementation of MB_BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE as described in the BAPI documentation?

  • Look at the sample implementation BAdI CL_EX_IM_MB_BAPI_GOODSMVT_CRE, which processes structures BAPI_TE_XMKPF and BAPI_TE_XMSEG.
  • Ensure that customer fields appended to tables MKPF and MSEG are also appended to structures IMKPF and IMSEG (see BAdI documentation).

NB: Copying/modifying standard code is usually (always) bad practice, apart from the risk of database discrepancies, it will cause problems with any patch or upgrade.

entry.title: Please let me know CDS views for extractor 0CO_OM_NWA_2 and 0CO_OM_WBS_6
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Please let me know CDS views which we can used instead of extractor's 0CO_OM_NWA_2 and 0CO_OM_WBS_6

Thanks

Vijay

entry.title: Re: Sales KIT
entry.content:
I will recommend to go with Sales BoM. 

Even if you have 100's of combinations, it is one time activity to create the Master Data (Sales BoM) and use the respective Header Material in the Sales order to explode the BoM and have the Items. 

Sales BoM will also be effective in handling Pricing / stock and optimizing the Delivery / Invoice Outputs. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Upload Prices in Sales from template-based spreadsheet
entry.content:
Hi, 

We have created the access sequence and it is available for selection in the filters, but the template does not show the new fields. Is there something that is missing? what should I check?

I checked your other blog on the extensibility, and I'm not sure if our consultants used the Custom Fields app to create everything. How do I proceed?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Askb
entry.content:
Didn't the transaction fill an apllication log?

Look with ARAL or SLG1 for 

ObjectFIAAFixed Assets Accounting
SubObject006Periodic Posting of APC Values

entry.title: How to create migration objects in PROD system.
entry.content:
Hello SAP support team,

Could you pls refer to standard and recommended SAP guideline of setting PROD system? Would be great to get SAP notes for creation of the migration objects in PROD system.

 

It is suggested to create migration objects in this order :

1.Bank

2.CO-Profit Center

3.CO-Profit Center (no clue why duplicate)

4.MM-supplier for contingent worker

5.EMP - employee

6.MM - Supplier

 

Our SAP partner requires to create their external accounts as internal customer users Employee (BUP003), because there is no other way how to create migration objects and check in PROD system.

The intention is to deactivate these accounts after PROD setup. However, are these accounts permanent or will we be able to delete them from PROD system?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create migration objects in PROD system.
entry.content:
Thank you for reaching out and for your patience as we address your concern.

For comprehensive information on 'available migration objects,' including those you have mentioned such as Bank, CO-Profit Center, MM-Supplier for Contingent Worker, Employee, and MM-Supplier, we recommend visiting the SAP Help Portal. This resource provides detailed guidelines on the creation order, roles, and authorizations needed for each object.

You can find the SAP Help Portal at the following link: [Available Migration Objects | SAP Help Portal]. In the portal, you will find dedicated sections that cover each of the migration objects that you have mentioned and others.

More information about Data Migration is also available in this Help Portal: [Data Migration | SAP Help Portal]

 

In relation to your query about the accounts that are needed in order for setup, 'Employee - BUP003' is a method to manage migration objects and although the accounts are permanent they can be deactivated after PROD setup by ensuring the 'Validity Date' is only active during the necessary period.

 

If you have any further questions or need additional assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me.

entry.title: Re: BRS (Bank Reconciliation Statement)
entry.content:
Hi @joerg_rolapp ,

The standard for cheques in public edition is set as Cheque In (+++++60 g/l) and Cheque Out (+++++50 g/l). Similarly, I have created Bank Transfer In (11010010 g/l). My question is, what is the external transaction key for Bank Transfer In to be processed in the Manage Bank Statements app?

I couldn't find the external transaction key for Bank Transfer In either, so I created a new external transaction key, 030, by copying from the 070 transaction key and assigned it to the posting rule F002. Is this process correct? Please find the screenshot below.

Note: For Bank Transfer Out standard in public cloud is +++++020 and external transaction key is 020.

entry.title: Re: Solution order management
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, implementing business logic and validations like the one you described can typically be achieved using a combination of business configuration and extensions.

Here's a high-level approach to enforce the rule where a service item can only be processed after the corresponding sales item is marked as "Complete":

1. **Business Process Configuration**: Start by reviewing the standard configuration settings in SAP Solution Order Management. See if there are options to define dependencies between sales items and service items, though such specific dependencies might not be available out-of-the-box.

2. **Custom Business Logic with Business Rules**:
- Utilize the SAP Business Rules framework to define custom rules. You can create a rule that checks the status of the corresponding sales item when a service item is attempted to be processed.
- The rule should evaluate the status of the sales item and, based on the condition that the sales item is not complete, it should trigger an error message.

3. **Validation Using CDS Views and BAdIs**:
- Create a CDS (Core Data Services) view that joins the data for both the sales and service items and their statuses. This can help you identify the completion status of both items.
- Use BAdIs (Business Add-Ins) to implement the custom logic that checks the sales item status when attempting to process the service item. You can code the logic to throw an error when the condition is not met.

4. **Utilizing SAP Cloud Platform Extensions**: If the above methods do not meet the requirements, consider developing a custom application on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) that interfaces with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, performs the validation, and prevents processing as needed.

5. **In-app Extensibility (Custom Logic/Enhancements)**:
- Use the Extensibility Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to create a custom enhancement that adds the validation logic as per your requirements.
- Implement a custom logic extension in the service item processing step to check if the corresponding sales item status is marked as complete before allowing further processing.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Restricting Input Box to Accept Only Numeric Data in...
entry.content:
The best I can find is to use the "Set Component Properties" flow function with the onChange event of the input field.

You set the component's value field with the following formula:

IF(MATCHES_REGEX(SUBSTRING(pageVars.input,-1), "[0-9]"),pageVars.input,SUBSTRING(pageVars.input,0,-1))

 It checks for a digit at the end and if not it removes the character. The "input" variable is a text field -- you'll have to convert it when you want to use as a number.

I still get a small flash of the character and its removal, but it's pretty quick.

Our old friend @kleventcov (Kirill 😺) has a wonderful blog on validation schemes which are worth checking out. It includes validation on submit though not on individual field input: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/moderating-user-input-in-sap-build-apps/ba-p/13558659

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM – APJ edition
entry.content:
The monthly webinar - focusing on the APJ region and timezone - provides everything you need to know about SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest news and regional updates directly from SAP's topic experts!

By attending this call you have the chance to stay updated about what's going on with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run.

For slides and recordings click here. 

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: Re: Corporate-to-Bank Connectivity in SAP: An Overview
entry.content: Excellent Blog to understand the SAP connectivity with Banking Sector ..

entry.title: Re: In SAP Business One, how can I check how many items h...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

In this case you can write a query that search all possible marketing documents. I will give an example of RDR1 (Sales Order). Then you can replicate the same logic for other marketing documents.

SELECT count(*),

               T2."firstName"

FROM ORDR T0

INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T1."DocEntry" = T0."DocEntry"

INNER JOIN OHEM T2 ON T0."OwnerCode" = T2."empID"

GROUP BY T2."firstName"

 

Additionally you can adjust the query to fit your scenario (e.g exclude canceled documents, include other marketing documents; maybe transform in a count widget per user - that shows only their documents; or a dashboard, or a formmatted search to be user like a report).

I don't know all the details of your scenario but that is a start point because a default functionality for your requirement in SAP B1 is unknown to me.

 

Best regards

entry.content:
Hi again, getting back to this topic, as I do not seem to get good answers to this from SAP or other KBA's:
When doing a full resync job, are passwords created in IAS lost? Since a resync job removes and adds all users again.

We have internal users from our AzureAD, they will be OK I guess, since password is managed elsewhere.
But internal users without AzureAd where password is set only in IAS.
Or external learners from LMS where password is only set in IAS. 
What happens to those users in IAS after a full resync job? Do they all have to set a new password?

entry.title: Re: BAPI / Simulate API alternative for BAPI: BAPI_PRICES...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please pay attention that a new feature is released in 2502 - Manage Price Agreements for Sales Contracts, if that is relevant. Here is the link to my video on this feature: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=75boOKx732Q

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the concept of BAPIs as used in ECC is generally replaced by OData services and APIs. For your requirement of simulating price condition data changes before actually making the POST or PUT call, you can use the "Price Simulation - Read" service.

This service, known as API_PURGDOCPRCGSIMULATION, allows you to simulate the net price for a transaction based on a pricing schema. It doesn't directly change or create records but instead provides a simulation capability that can help you predict the outcome of your intended changes before you actually commit them.

Here's how you can approach your requirement:

1. **Preparation:** Prepare your data in an Excel file as per your business requirements for mass creation or change.

2. **Simulation:** Use the "Price Simulation - Read" API to simulate the net price changes based on your proposed data. This allows you to test the data before uploading it permanently.

3. **Execution:** Once you are satisfied with the simulation results, proceed to actually create or change the price conditions using the relevant API for creating or updating pricing records.

4. **Communication Scenario:** Ensure that you have configured the required communication scenario, specifically SAP_COM_0294, to use these pricing and simulation services.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Standard BAPI BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE copy

entry.title: Re: AATP oprions for manual allocation/re-allocation
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA, Advanced Available-to-Promise (AATP) provides several features that can help you manage and optimize the allocation of quantities to different sales orders. Given your situation in the automotive industry with fluctuating demands and urgent requests, AATP can be a highly beneficial tool for managing these challenges.

To address your specific question regarding reallocating ATP quantities from one sales order to another, SAP AATP has functionalities like Backorder Processing (BOP) and Product Allocation (PAL) which can be very useful:

1. **Backorder Processing (BOP):** This feature allows you to re-evaluate and reschedule existing sales orders based on current priorities and business rules. With BOP, you can reprioritize sales orders and reallocate available quantities to higher-priority orders. This means that if a more urgent order comes in, you can adjust your existing allocations to ensure critical deliveries are met.

2. **Product Allocation (PAL):** PAL is used to manage and control the distribution of limited product quantities to various customers or regions. It can help you prioritize certain customers or orders based on predefined criteria, ensuring that critical demands are met first.

Both BOP and PAL enable you to dynamically adjust to changing demands and reallocate resources efficiently. Given your high lead times and the need to prevent production line stoppages, these tools can be essential for ensuring that ATP allocations align with business priorities.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: EML: Read All Instances of a BO

entry.title: Re: Error: Document XXXX has been saved (pricing error) i...
entry.content:
Hello,

When encountering a pricing error in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud while using the SOAP API "billingdocumentrequest_in," even though the billing document is saved, here are some considerations and steps you can take to troubleshoot and resolve the issue:

1. **Specific Pricing Issues:**
- **Missing Condition Records:** The pricing procedure could be missing condition records needed for calculation. Ensure that all relevant conditions have corresponding records maintained.
- **Incorrect Access Sequences:** If access sequences are incorrectly configured, the system may not be able to determine the right condition records.
- **Inactive or Deleted Conditions:** Check to ensure that necessary conditions are not inactive or mistakenly deleted.
- **Price Calculation Errors:** There could be a calculation error due to incorrect formulas or invalid data inputs.

2. **Identifying the Exact Pricing Condition:**
- **Pricing Analysis Tool:** Use the pricing analysis tools available in SAP to evaluate the pricing conditions that were accessed during document creation. This can help identify which condition records were missing or had issues.
- **Condition Type Check:** Verify all condition types in the pricing procedure to ensure they are configured correctly and are active.
- **Condition Record Review:** Look into condition records for validity periods, scales, and assigned access sequences to pinpoint any gaps.

3. **Common Configuration Gaps to Recheck:**
- **Access Sequence Configuration:** Confirm that access sequences are set up properly and linked correctly to the condition types.
- **Condition Record Completeness:** Ensure all condition records needed for the pricing procedure are correctly maintained.
- **Currency and Unit Consistencies:** Check that all values are maintained consistently in the expected units and currencies.

4. **Debugging Tips in SAP Public Cloud:**
- **Error Logs and Trace Tools:** Use the error logs and any available trace tools in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to find detailed error messages related to pricing.
- **Simulate Pricing:** Use the simulation options if available to see how pricing behaves without actually creating a billing document.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Cannot login to Power Designer Portal using ADMIN credentials anymore
entry.content:
Hello,

We setup the PowerDesigner Portal more than one year ago.
The ADMIN password that we used to login to the portal is no longer working: "Invalid username and/or password. Please try again."

Can someone help?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cannot login to Power Designer Portal using ADMIN cre...
entry.content:
Hello,

The user is probably blocked and the cmrserver.log file should clearly indicate why ADMIN could not connect anymore.
As an example, "The user 'ADMIN' is blocked.Please contact your system administrator."

Any other member of the Administrators group in the Repository's list of users can change the password of ADMIN.
If ADMIN is the only administrator for the repository then you have to follow the steps described in KBA 2542533 to reset the password that unblocks ADMIN.

Kind regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Convert Product to GenericVariantProduct
entry.content: Good Morning,

Thanks,

Smriti Gupta

entry.title: Re: Custom Price Value Not Updated in Credit Memo Request...
entry.content:
Hello,

You may refer to this blog on this topic:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/import-credit-memo-requests-introduction/ba-p/13957016

When working with credit memo requests and having issues with custom pricing conditions not updating as expected, especially in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment, there are a few considerations and possible approaches to explore:

1. **Pricing Procedure Configuration**: Ensure that the pricing procedure used in the credit memo request is correctly configured to handle custom condition types like ZUDE. Check if the pricing procedure is correctly assigned and includes your custom condition types and pricing routines.

2. **Condition Control**: The condition control for the custom condition type ZUDE may be causing the issue. You mentioned trying to change it but didn't succeed. Review the configuration of the condition type and verify if any restrictions prevent it from being updated. Condition control setup can impact how certain elements are recalculated or updated.

3. **Copy Control Settings**: The issue might be related to the copy control settings from billing to credit memo request. Review the configuration for item copy control and ensure that the relevant settings allow for proper copying and updating of pricing conditions.

4. **Pricing Type**: You noted that "Pricing Type C" (copy manual price elements and redetermine others) isn't available in the system customizing in the Public Cloud. This limitation might require alternative strategies, like custom logic through available BADIs (Business Add-Ins).

5. **BADI Utilization**: The use of BADI `PRCG_DOC_ITEM_CONDITION_AMOUNT` should be explored further to modify or enhance the behavior of the pricing conditions. Double-check the implementation logic here to ensure it correctly affects the custom condition type ZUDE based on your requirements.

6. **Custom Pricing Routine**: Ensure that the custom pricing routine assigned to ZUDE is working as intended. You might want to debug or log the routine's execution to verify that it is being triggered and calculated correctly.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Database vendor code 18 error message when working with Crystal report 2016 and TLS 1.2
entry.content:
We have ASP .NET web application which uses Crystal report services to generate the report. We have .rpt file for each type of our report and it generates the PDF report. We are using Crystal report 2016 on Windows server 2016 and .NET 4.8. The Crystal report works fine with TLS 1.0 and but its not working with TLS 1.2. When tried to generate the report with TLS 1.2 then we are getting 'SSL Security error SQL state 08001. Native error 18. [Database vendor code: 18]' error message.

Here is code snippet of VB .NET code file where the database details are passed to .rpt file to generate the report.

 

This is the error message we are getting -

Do we need to upgrade to Crystal report 2020 to resolve this issue? Let us know any solution for this issue.

entry.title: DIfference PA0105-SUBTY and PA0105-USRTY
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I'm really try to wrap my head around the two above mentioned fields in table PA0105.

I don't find any different values between the two in our complete dataset and it seems to me that SUBTY is a keyfield of the table whereas USRTY is "simply" an inserted value.

Can anyone perhaps enlighten me as to why these two fields exists in the same table while holding identical values?

entry.title: Re: JSON Message to change field value to null via Groovy...
entry.content:
Hi @ZTK196

Work with JSON in Groovy is easy with the classes:

import groovy.json.JsonSlurper -> Process a JSON file
import groovy.json.JsonOutput -> Generate a new JSON file
 
Here you have the code for your example:
 
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
import groovy.json.JsonOutput

def Message processData(Message message) {
   
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
    def slurper = new groovy.json.JsonSlurper()
    def jsonInput = slurper.parseText(body)
   
    def transformedJson = [
    Book: [
        Title: convertNil(jsonInput.Book.Title),
        Author: convertNil(jsonInput.Book.Author),
        year: convertNil(jsonInput.Book.year),
        isbn: convertNil(jsonInput.Book.isbn)
    ]
]

    def outputJson = JsonOutput.prettyPrint(JsonOutput.toJson(transformedJson))
           
    message.setBody(outputJson);
    return message;
}

def convertNil(obj) {
    if (obj instanceof Map && obj.containsKey('@nil')) {
        return obj['@nil'] == "true" ? null : obj['@nil']
    }
    return obj
}
 
Hope it helps.
Regards,
Roberto

entry.title: Deactivating SAP Credit Management Checks in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
• Do not want to use the SAP Credit Management to check the sales order check.
  • SAP Credit management should not block the creation of sales orders.
  • Sales orders are credit checked despite the un assignment of UKM000 role and credit segment to the customer.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Deactivating SAP Credit Management Checks in S/4HANA...
entry.content: Please find the solution given in SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3567229
